515	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
516	
517	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
518	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
519	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,

515	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
516	
517	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
518	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
519	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
520	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
521	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
522	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
523	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
524	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
525	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
526	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
527	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
528	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
529	  intro x
530	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
531	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
532	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
533	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
534	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
535	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
536	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
537	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
538	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
539	    tensorObj_unit_iso
540	
541	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
542	
543	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
544	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
545	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
546	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
547	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
548	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
549	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
550	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
551	
552	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
553	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
554	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
555	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
556	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
557	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
558	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
559	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
560	    IsIso φ := by
561	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
562	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
563	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
564	    intro x
565	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
566	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
567	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
568	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
569	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
570	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
571	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
572	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
573	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
574	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
575	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
576	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
577	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
578	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
579	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
580	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
581	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
582	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
583	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
584	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
585	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
586	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
587	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
588	    exact this
589	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
590	
591	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
592	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
593	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
594	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
595	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
596	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
597	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
598	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
599	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
600	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
601	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
602	    M ⟶ N :=
603	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
604	
605	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
606	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
607	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
608	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
609	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
610	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
611	  rfl

630	    Sheaf (gt ↥U) A`; the residual is a `PresheafOfModules`-level iso (Step 3 already

919	lemma evalLin_smul
920	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
921	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
922	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
923	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
924	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
925	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
926	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
927	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
928	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
929	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
930	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
931	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
932	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
933	  rfl
934	
935	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
936	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
937	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
938	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
939	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
940	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
941	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
942	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
943	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
944	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
945	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
946	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
947	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
948	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
949	  ModuleCat.ofHom
950	    (TensorProduct.lift
951	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
952	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
953	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
954	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
955	        (fun s φ φ' => by
956	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
957	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
958	        (fun c s φ => by
959	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
960	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))
961	
962	/-- Value of `internalHomEvalApp` on a simple tensor: the contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`.
963	The eval value is kept at its NATURAL over-ring type `R₀.obj X` (not ascribed to the
964	unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`). Used to reduce the naturality of `internalHomEval`. -/
965	@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
966	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
967	    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
968	    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
969	  rfl
970	
971	/-- **Further-restriction of `M|_U` along the canonical `Over.homMk f.unop` equals `M.map f`.**
972	For `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Dᵒᵖ`, restricting `M|_{X.unop}` (the `pushforward₀` of `M` along
973	`Over.forget X.unop`) along the over-category morphism `Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶
974	Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)` is, definitionally, `M.map f`. Extracted as its own lemma so the heavy
975	`whnf` defeq runs once within its own heartbeat budget (the `internalHomEval` naturality proof
976	would otherwise time out). Used to rewrite the `naturality_apply` of a dual section into the
977	shape `ev_M`'s naturality needs. -/
978	private lemma restr_map_homMk
979	    (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
980	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
981	      = N.map f := rfl
982	
983	/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):
984	the full natural morphism `M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, with `app X := internalHomEvalApp M X`.
985	CLOSED axiom-clean iter-224 (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`). The per-object/value content
986	is `internalHomEvalApp` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`; the `naturality` field is the six-step
987	`evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction recorded in the proof body. The iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB
988	diagnosis (>200000 heartbeats forced by `kabstract` whnf-ing the monoidal unit `𝟙_`) turned out to be
989	STALE after a Mathlib update: the composition now splits cleanly with
990	`erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, every elementwise rewrite (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`,
991	`PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply`, `restr_map_homMk`) fires without any bomb, and the dual-section
992	naturality square closes directly. No transparency hacks (`with_reducible`), no `unit`-reshape, and no
993	`maxHeartbeats` bump were needed. -/
994	noncomputable def internalHomEval
995	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
996	    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
997	      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
998	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
999	    intro X Y f
1000	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
1001	    -- iter-224: the iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing
1002	    -- `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit machinery, >200000 heartbeats) turned out STALE after a
1003	    -- Mathlib update. The composition splits cleanly with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]` and every
1004	    -- elementwise rewrite fires without any bomb; the six-step `evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction below
1005	    -- goes through directly. No `with_reducible`, no `unit`-reshape, no `maxHeartbeats` bump.
1006	    -- Step 1: break the two `≫` on `s ⊗ₜ φ`, then `tensorObj_map_tmul` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`
1007	    -- (handled by `erw`'s defeq matching through `restrictScalars`):
1008	    --   LHS ⇒ `evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) ((M.map f) s)`.
1009	    erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
1010	      internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
1011	    simp only []
1012	    -- Reduce the RHS `(𝟙_).map f`-image of `(internalHomEvalApp M X)(s ⊗ₜ φ)` to the unit ring
1013	    -- map applied to `evalLin M X φ s` (defeq, since `internalHomEvalApp_tmul` is `rfl`).
1014	    change M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) ((M.map f) s)
1015	      = ((𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
1016	          (M.evalLin X φ s)
1017	    -- Step 4: naturality of the dual section `φ` (a morphism over `Over X.unop`) along the
1018	    -- canonical `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : (terminal) ⟶ (op (Over.mk f.unop))`, applied to `s`.
1019	    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
1020	      (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1021	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1022	      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
1023	    -- Both further-restrictions `(restr X.unop _).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op` are definitionally
1024	    -- the base maps `M.map f` / `(𝟙_).map f` (`restr_map_homMk`).
1025	    rw [restr_map_homMk, restr_map_homMk] at key
1026	    -- Step 5 (`hdt`): identify the dual section's terminal value with `φ` evaluated at
1027	    -- `op (Over.mk f.unop)` (the over-objects `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)` and `Over.mk f.unop`
1028	    -- are equal via `Category.id_comp`, with equal source `Y.unop`, so `hom_app_heq`+`eq_of_heq`).
1029	    have hdt : M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
1030	      congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
1031	        (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq
1032	          (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1033	            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1034	          (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))
1035	    -- Step 6: chain `hdt` (applied to `(M.map f) s`) into `key`.
1036	    exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key
1037	    )

1039	/-- **Precomposition `R(U)`-linear equivalence on dual sections induced by an iso `e : M ≅ M'`.**

225	mirrors `tensorObj_restrict_iso` exactly (Step 1 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Step 2
226	`sheafificationCompPullback`, Step 3 strip sheafification, Step 4 H1
227	`pushforwardPushforwardAdj`∘`leftAdjointUniq` ∘ this H2′), with `⊗` replaced by `dual`
228	and `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` replaced by this equivalence. Like its tensor
229	counterpart it is **pure algebra over a ring iso — no stalk, no filtered-colimit, no
230	whiskering of the sheafification unit (no d.2)**: a map `M → S` is `R`-linear (via `e`)
231	iff it is `S`-linear, and `e.symm` carries the codomain `S` to `R`. The forward map is
232	`R`-linear; the inverse `ψ ↦ e ∘ ψ` is its two-sided inverse. The `iMS` `Module R`
233	instance on `M →ₗ[S] S` is base change along `e`. -/
234	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
235	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] :
236	    letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
237	    letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
238	    (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[R] R) := by
239	  letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
240	  letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
241	  have hsmulM : ∀ (r : R) (m : M), r • m = e r • m := fun _ _ => rfl
242	  refine
243	    { toFun := fun φ =>
244	        { toFun := fun m => e.symm (φ m)
245	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
246	          map_smul' := fun r m => by
247	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
248	            rw [hsmulM, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
249	      invFun := fun ψ =>
250	        { toFun := fun m => e (ψ m)
251	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
252	          map_smul' := fun s m => by
253	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
254	            have hsm : s • m = (e.symm s) • m := by rw [hsmulM, e.apply_symm_apply]
255	            rw [hsm, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.apply_symm_apply] }
256	      left_inv := fun φ => by ext m; exact e.apply_symm_apply _
257	      right_inv := fun ψ => by ext m; exact e.symm_apply_apply _
258	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => by
259	        ext m
260	        simp only [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, map_add]
261	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
262	        ext m
263	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
264	          smul_eq_mul]
265	        change e.symm ((e r • φ) m) = r * e.symm (φ m)
266	        rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
267	
268	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor

270	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
271	
272	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
273	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
274	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
275	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
276	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
277	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
278	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
279	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
280	
281	namespace PresheafOfModules
282	
283	universe v'
284	
285	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
286	
287	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
288	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
289	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
290	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
291	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
292	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
293	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
294	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
295	  naturality {X Y} f := by
296	    ext r
297	    dsimp
298	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
299	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
300	    simp only [map_one]
301	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
302	
303	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
304	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
305	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
306	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
307	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
308	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
309	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
310	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
311	  app X := by
312	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
313	  naturality {X Y} f := by
314	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)

1039	/-- **Precomposition `R(U)`-linear equivalence on dual sections induced by an iso `e : M ≅ M'`.**
1040	At an object `U`, sends a dual section `φ : M'|_U ⟶ R|_U` to `(e.hom|_U) ≫ φ : M|_U ⟶ R|_U`, with
1041	inverse `ψ ↦ (e.inv|_U) ≫ ψ`. The `R(U)`-action on dual sections is post-composition with
1042	`globalSMul` (the `homModule` action), which commutes with this pre-composition, so the map is
1043	`R(U)`-linear. This is the section-level core of `dualIsoOfIso` (the dual is contravariantly
1044	functorial in isomorphisms). -/
1045	noncomputable def dualPrecompEquiv
1046	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') (U : D) :
1047	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1048	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1049	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1050	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1051	    (restr U M' ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1052	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op U) : Type u)]
1053	      (restr U M ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) := by
1054	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1055	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1056	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1057	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1058	  exact
1059	    { toFun := fun φ =>
1060	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1061	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ
1062	      invFun := fun ψ =>
1063	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1064	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ
1065	      map_add' := fun φ ψ => Preadditive.comp_add _ _ _ _ φ ψ
1066	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
1067	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1068	        exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
1069	      left_inv := fun φ => by
1070	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1071	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv
1072	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1073	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ) = φ
1074	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.inv_hom_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1075	          Category.id_comp]
1076	      right_inv := fun ψ => by
1077	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1078	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom
1079	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1080	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ) = ψ
1081	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1082	          Category.id_comp] }
1083	
1084	/-- **The presheaf dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
1085	`e : M ≅ M'` of presheaves of modules induces an isomorphism `dual M' ≅ dual M` of their
1086	duals (`dual N = internalHom N (𝟙_)`), assembled sectionwise from the precomposition
1087	equivalences `dualPrecompEquiv`. This is the `\leanok`-ready ingredient that transports a
1088	local trivialisation `L|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` to `dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U` in the assembly of
1089	`dual_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1090	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso
1091	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') :
1092	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
1093	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk
1094	    (fun U => (dualPrecompEquiv e U.unop).toModuleIso)
1095	
1096	end Dual
1097	
1098	end PresheafOfModules
1099

628	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
629	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
630	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
631	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
632	  mul_smul f g φ := by
633	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
634	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
635	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
636	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
637	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
638	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
639	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
640	  add_smul f g φ := by
641	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
642	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
643	
644	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
645	
646	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
647	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
648	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
649	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
650	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
651	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
652	applied to that terminal. -/
653	
654	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
655	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
656	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
657	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
658	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
659	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
660	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
661	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
662	
663	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**

665	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions

669	@[implicit_reducible]

671	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
672	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=

674	    (restr U M) (restr U N)

676	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
677	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
678	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
679	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
680	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
681	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
682	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
683	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
684	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
685	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
686	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
687	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
688

690	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
691	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
692	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
693	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
694	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
695	  ext1 X; rfl
696	
697	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
698	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
699	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
700	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
701	  ext1 X; rfl
702	
703	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*

707	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
708	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
709	  subst h; rfl
710	
711	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
712	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
713	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
714	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
715	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
716	  ext1 X
717	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
718	
719	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
720	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
721	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
722	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
723	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
724	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
725	  ext1 X
726	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
727	
728	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
729	`pushforward₀.map`). -/

731	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
732	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :

noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal

-- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
  -- Residual: `(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`.

/-- **The dual of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf.** `dual 𝒪_Y ≅ 𝒪_Y`.
The presheaf-level dual of the monoidal unit `𝟙_` is the unit (evaluation at `1`),
sheafified and identified with the (already-sheaf) unit by the sheafification counit.
Mirrors `tensorObj_unit_iso` with the presheaf left unitor replaced by
`presheafDualUnitIso`. The third leg of the `dual_isLocallyTrivial` chain. -/
noncomputable def dual_unit_iso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    dual (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      (PresheafOfModules.presheafDualUnitIso (R₀ := Y.presheaf)) ≪≫
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
      (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

lemma dual_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (dual L) := by
  -- Mirrors `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`: trivialise the dual on each affine open `U`
  -- where `L` is trivial, via the three-step chain
  --   `(dual L)|_U ≅ dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U ≅ 𝒪_U`.
  intro x
  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eL⟩⟩ := hL x
  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
  exact dual_restrict_iso U.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eL).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso

819	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
820	
821	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
822	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
823	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
824	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
825	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
826	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
827	
828	namespace InternalHom
829	
830	section Assembly

837	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
838	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
839	section note for the universe constraint. -/
840	noncomputable def internalHom
841	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
842	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
843	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
844	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
845	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
846	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
847	
848	end Assembly

862	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the

578	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=

585	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
586	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
587	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
588	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
589	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)

596	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl

599	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by

Explanation: The rewrite tactic rewrites an expression 'e' using an equality 'a = b' by the following process. First, it looks for all 'a' in 'e'. Second, it tries to abstract these occurrences of 'a' to create a function 'm := fun _a => ...', called the *motive*, with the property that 'm a' is definitionally equal to 'e'. Third, we observe that 'congrArg' implies that 'm a = m b', which can be used with lemmas such as 'Eq.mpr' to change the goal. However, if 'e' depends on specific properties of 'a', then the motive 'm' might not typecheck.

Possible solutions: use rewrite's 'occs' configuration option to limit which occurrences are rewritten, or use 'simp' or 'conv' mode, which have strategies for certain kinds of dependencies (these tactics can handle proofs and 'Decidable' instances whose types depend on the rewritten term, and 'simp' can apply user-defined '@[congr]' theorems as well).

import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.PresheafInternalHom

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory Opposite

namespace PresheafOfModules

open InternalHom

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- Section equiv: endomorphisms of the (restricted) unit ≃ the ring, via eval at 1. -/
noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  exact
    { toFun := fun φ =>
        evalLin (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ 1
      map_add' := fun φ φ' => by rw [evalLin_add]; rfl
      map_smul' := fun c φ => by rw [evalLin_smul]; rfl
      invFun := fun r =>
        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
      left_inv := fun φ => by sorry
      right_inv := fun r => by sorry }

end PresheafOfModules

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit

Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/

namespace PresheafOfModules

open InternalHom Opposite

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  exact
    { toFun := fun φ =>
        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      invFun := fun r =>
        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
      left_inv := fun φ => by
        ext Y
        dsimp only
        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
        rfl
      right_inv := fun r => by
        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
            (restr X.unop
              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
        exact mul_one r }

/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
    (fun {X Y} f => by
      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      have hrm : (restr X.unop
            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
      rw [hrm] at key
      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)

end PresheafOfModules

# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean

Lane TS-inv (iter-251). Sorry count: **3 → 2**. `dual_isLocallyTrivial` CLOSED; the
`dual_unit_iso` leg fully built (incl. the new axiom-clean `presheafDualUnitIso`);
`dual_restrict_iso` advanced to its identified Step-4 residual.

## presheafDualUnitIso / dualUnitIsoGen / unitDualSectionEquiv (new §0 block) — RESOLVED
### Approach
The third leg of the `dual_isLocallyTrivial` chain needs `dual 𝒪_Y ≅ 𝒪_Y`, which sheafifies
the **presheaf-level** `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_` (= `ℋom(𝟙_,𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`, evaluation at `1`).
Built generally over `{D}[Category.{u,u} D]{R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}` so it instantiates at
`R₀ := Y.presheaf`.

- `unitDualSectionEquiv X : (restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_) ≃ₗ[R₀(X)] R₀(X)` — the section equiv.
  - forward `φ ↦ evalLin 𝟙_ X φ 1`; `map_add' := rfl`; `map_smul'` via `evalLin_smul`.
  - inverse `r ↦ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X 𝟙_) r` (mult by a global scalar).
  - `right_inv` = `globalSMul_hom_apply` + `termRingMap_terminal` + `mul_one`.
  - **`left_inv` (the substantive content)** — every unit-endomorphism is mult by its value at
    `1`. Proof: `ext Y; erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]`; then `φ`-naturality toward the terminal
    `(Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op` (`naturality_apply` + `unit_map_one`) gives
    `φ.app Y 1 = termRingMap Y (evalLin φ 1)`; `erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]; rfl`.
- `dualUnitIsoGen` = `PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso) <nat>`.
  The **naturality** mirrors `InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s six-step reduction specialised to
  `M = 𝟙_`, `s = 1`: `naturality_apply φ (Over.homMk f.unop).op 1`, the rfl-lemma
  `restr X.unop 𝟙_ .map (Over.homMk f.unop).op = 𝟙_.map f` (inlined; the private `restr_map_homMk`),
  `unit_map_one`, and `hdt` via an inlined `hom_app_heq` (`HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B)` by `subst`),
  closing with `(DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key`.

### Verification
Developed + **verified axiom-clean in an isolated scratch file** importing only
`PresheafInternalHom.lean` (the only dependency of §0). Scratch compiled with **0 warnings /
0 errors / 0 sorry**, then ported verbatim and the scratch deleted. `presheafDualUnitIso` is now
just `dualUnitIsoGen (R₀ := Y.presheaf)`.

### Key idioms (for reuse)
- The CommRingCat/RingCat carrier friction blocks `LinearMap.add_apply`/generic lemmas; `evalLin_add`
  is `rfl`-level so `map_add' := rfl`. `erw` (not `rw`) is required for `globalSMul_hom_apply`,
  `unit_map_one` (the `𝟙_` vs `unit` defeq) through `.app/.hom`.
- `1` must be ascribed `(1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))` to dodge the
  `OfNat` synthesis failure through the ModuleCat coercion.
- The internal-hom module on the bare hom-type isn't auto-found: pin it with
  `letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop 𝟙_ 𝟙_`, build the `LinearEquiv` over `R₀.obj (op X.unop)`
  (mirroring the closed `dualPrecompEquiv`), then `.toModuleIso`.

## dual_unit_iso — RESOLVED
`(sheafification ...).mapIso presheafDualUnitIso ≪≫ (asIso counit).app (unit)` — the exact
`tensorObj_unit_iso` pattern with the presheaf left-unitor replaced by `presheafDualUnitIso`.

exact dual_restrict_iso U.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eL).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso
```
The three-step chain `(dual L)|_U ≅ dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U ≅ 𝒪_U`, mirroring
`tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. Closed (no sorry in its body); references `dual_restrict_iso` (a def
with the Step-4 residual sorry). Verified clean (LSP) before the parallel-lane perturbation.

## dual_restrict_iso — PARTIAL (Steps 1–3 + H1 done; Step-4 residual identified)
Mirrors `tensorObj_restrict_iso`:

  sheafification (`.mapIso`) — all typecheck (LSP-verified), descending to the presheaf residual.

## homOfLocalCompat — OPEN (not attempted this iter)
Untouched (still the scaffolded `sorry`). I prioritised the dual-iso chain (which closed cleanly:
